items.title: Supervised learning losses
items.description: Cross-entropy, Bernoulli NLL, Poisson deviance, and Gaussian NLL are negative log-likelihoods under chosen models; training maximizes the product likelihood over the training set.

items.title: Parameter estimation (MLE)
items.description: Sample proportion for Bernoulli, sample mean for Gaussian μ, and softmax weights in logistic regression all arise from maximizing L(θ) or ℓ(θ).

items.title: Generative classifiers
items.description: Naive Bayes (Bernoulli, Gaussian, Multinomial) ranks classes by prior × product of class-conditional likelihoods P(x | class, θ).

items.title: Bayesian inference
items.description: Likelihood P(data | θ) combines with a prior in MCMC, variational Bayes, and conjugate updates to form posterior beliefs over parameters.

items.title: Model comparison
items.description: Likelihood ratios, AIC, and BIC use maximized log-likelihood to compare nested or competing models on the same data.
